reference: GATE CS — Probability and Statistics / Probability

map.condition: Fixed n trials, each success prob p, count successes
Count events in fixed time/space, rate \lambda
Waiting for first success, geometric trials

formulas.name: Binomial Distribution — PMF
formulas.whenToUse: n independent Bernoulli trials, each with success probability p; count total successes
formulas.trap: Trials must be independent and p must be constant; without replacement changes it to Hypergeometric

formulas.name: Binomial — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Computing expected successes or spread in n Bernoulli trials
formulas.trap: Standard deviation is sqrt(np(1-p)), not np(1-p); confusing variance and std-dev loses marks

formulas.name: Poisson Distribution — PMF
formulas.whenToUse: Counting rare events in a fixed interval when the average rate lambda is known

terms.means: average number of events per interval (rate)

formulas.trap: Poisson approximates Binomial when n is large and p is small with lambda = np; do not use Binomial formula in that setting

formulas.name: Poisson — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Both mean and variance equal the rate parameter — a distinctive property
formulas.trap: Mean equals variance only for Poisson; if a question states mean != variance the distribution is NOT Poisson

formulas.name: Geometric Distribution — PMF
formulas.whenToUse: Number of trials until (and including) the first success in independent Bernoulli trials

terms.means: trial on which first success occurs
probability of success on each trial

formulas.trap: Two conventions: X = trial of first success (starts at 1) vs. X = failures before first success (starts at 0); mean is 1/p in the first, (1-p)/p in the second

formulas.name: Geometric — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Expected number of trials for first success
formulas.trap: If p=0.5, mean is 2 trials, not 1; students often confuse the reciprocal

formulas.name: Memoryless Property of Geometric Distribution
formulas.latex: P(X > m+n \mid X > m) = P(X > n)
formulas.plain: P(X > m+n | X > m) = P(X > n)
formulas.whenToUse: Past failures do not affect future trial probabilities — only discrete distribution with this property
formulas.trap: This property belongs to Geometric (discrete) and Exponential (continuous); Binomial and Poisson do NOT have it

formulas.name: Hypergeometric Distribution — PMF
formulas.whenToUse: Sampling n items WITHOUT replacement from a finite population of N containing K successes

terms.means: number of success states in population

formulas.trap: Use Binomial when sampling WITH replacement; use Hypergeometric when sampling WITHOUT replacement

formulas.name: Bernoulli Distribution — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Single trial with two outcomes; building block for Binomial
formulas.trap: Variance p(1-p) is maximized at p=0.5, giving 0.25; students sometimes forget (1-p) factor

formulas.name: Uniform Distribution — PDF and CDF
formulas.whenToUse: All outcomes in [a,b] equally likely; e.g. random arrival in a time window

terms.means: lower and upper bounds of the interval

formulas.trap: Mean is (a+b)/2 not the midpoint of [0,1]; always substitute actual a and b

formulas.name: Uniform — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Computing expected value or spread of a uniform random variable
formulas.trap: Variance denominator is 12, not 2 or 4; a common arithmetic error in GATE

formulas.name: Normal Distribution — PDF
formulas.whenToUse: Continuous data that is symmetric and bell-shaped around the mean
formulas.trap: The parameter in N(mu, sigma^2) is variance sigma^2, not sigma; misidentifying leads to wrong standardization

formulas.name: Standardization (Z-score)
formulas.whenToUse: Converting any normal variable to standard normal to use Z-tables or compare distributions
formulas.trap: Divide by sigma (std dev), NOT sigma^2 (variance); this is the most frequent algebraic error

formulas.name: Normal Distribution — Empirical Rule
formulas.plain: 68% within 1 sigma, 95% within 2 sigma, 99.7% within 3 sigma
formulas.whenToUse: Quick probability estimates for normal distributions without a Z-table
formulas.trap: These are approximate values; GATE may expect exact values from Z-tables for precise answers

formulas.name: Exponential Distribution — PDF and CDF
formulas.plain: f(x) = lambda * e^(-lambda*x), CDF F(x) = 1 - e^(-lambda*x)
formulas.whenToUse: Modeling time between events in a Poisson process; waiting time
formulas.trap: Mean = 1/lambda, not lambda; students flip rate and mean

formulas.name: Exponential — Mean and Variance
formulas.whenToUse: Expected waiting time or spread in an exponential model
formulas.trap: Standard deviation equals the mean (1/lambda); exponential is the only common distribution with CV = 1

formulas.name: Memoryless Property of Exponential Distribution
formulas.latex: P(X > s+t \mid X > s) = P(X > t)
formulas.plain: P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t)
formulas.whenToUse: The remaining wait time does not depend on how long you have already waited
formulas.trap: Exponential is the ONLY continuous distribution with the memoryless property; Geometric is the discrete analogue

formulas.name: Linearity of Expectation
formulas.whenToUse: Always — holds for any random variables X and Y, independent or not
formulas.trap: E[XY] = E[X]*E[Y] only when X and Y are independent; do NOT apply this to products in general

formulas.name: Variance — Computational Formula
formulas.whenToUse: Computing variance from the second moment and mean
formulas.trap: E[X^2] != (E[X])^2 unless X is constant; always compute E[X^2] separately

formulas.name: Variance of Linear Transformation
formulas.whenToUse: Scaling or shifting a random variable; b (constant shift) does not affect variance
formulas.trap: The b constant disappears; students add b^2 to the variance, which is wrong

formulas.name: Variance of Sum of Independent Variables
formulas.plain: Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) when X and Y are independent
formulas.whenToUse: Summing independent random variables; variances add
formulas.trap: If X and Y are not independent, Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) + 2*Cov(X,Y)

formulas.name: Covariance and Correlation
formulas.whenToUse: Measuring linear relationship between two random variables

terms.means: standard deviations of X and Y

formulas.trap: Independent implies Cov=0, but Cov=0 does NOT imply independence (uncorrelated != independent)

formulas.name: Central Limit Theorem
formulas.plain: Sample mean X-bar converges in distribution to N(mu, sigma^2/n) as n -> infinity
formulas.whenToUse: Approximating distribution of sample mean for large n regardless of original distribution

terms.means: sample mean of n iid observations

formulas.trap: Variance of the sample mean is sigma^2/n not sigma^2; halving n does NOT halve the std error, it multiplies by sqrt(2)
formulas.reference: Probability and Statistics / Central Limit Theorem

formulas.name: Law of Large Numbers
formulas.plain: Sample mean converges in probability to population mean mu as n -> infinity
formulas.whenToUse: Justifying that long-run averages stabilize at the expected value
formulas.trap: LLN gives convergence of the mean, not of individual observations; outliers can still occur for any finite n

formulas.name: Markov's and Chebyshev's Inequalities
formulas.whenToUse: Bounding tail probabilities without knowing the exact distribution

terms.means: number of standard deviations from mean

formulas.trap: Markov requires X >= 0; Chebyshev's bound 1/k^2 is distribution-free but loose — normal distribution is much tighter
